<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] 27 files at the root is on the higher end; periodic review to ensure new scripts don't bypass the spawn-not-import convention is worthwhile.
[observation] [info] Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm the architectural boundary between orchestration scripts and domain packages is holding cleanly.
[suggestion] [warning] Add an eslint no-restricted-imports rule (or a lightweight CI grep check) to the root package that flags any static import/require from 'rex', 'sourcevision', 'hench', or '@n-dx/*' inside cli.js, ci.js, web.js, and config.js. This converts the architectural boundary from a documented convention into an enforced invariant.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- All cross-package orchestration is done by spawning child processes (cli.js, ci.js, web.js), correctly enforcing the zero-import boundary between orchestration and domain layers.
- The mix of orchestration scripts (ci.js, cli.js, web.js, config.js) and static config/docs at the root is intentional — this zone is the system's single coordination point.
- With cohesion 1.0 and coupling 0.0, this zone is maximally self-contained; no upstream consumers depend on it, consistent with its role as a terminal orchestration layer.
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm the architectural boundary between orchestration scripts and domain packages is holding cleanly.
- 27 files at the root is on the higher end; periodic review to ensure new scripts don't bypass the spawn-not-import convention is worthwhile.
- The spawn-not-import boundary between orchestration scripts and domain packages is enforced by convention only — no lint rule, TypeScript path restriction, or CI import guard exists to detect an accidental require() in cli.js, ci.js, or web.js. A single undetected violation would silently collapse the four-tier hierarchy.
- Add an eslint no-restricted-imports rule (or a lightweight CI grep check) to the root package that flags any static import/require from 'rex', 'sourcevision', 'hench', or '@n-dx/*' inside cli.js, ci.js, web.js, and config.js. This converts the architectural boundary from a documented convention into an enforced invariant.
- [call graph] 412 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
